summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

In a titration, what is the analyte?

The analyte is the solution of unknown concentration being analyzed. The titrant is added to the analyte.

What is the salt formed when HNO₃ reacts with Mg(OH)₂?

2HNO₃ + Mg(OH)₂ → Mg(NO₃)₂ + 2H₂O. The salt is magnesium nitrate: Mg(NO₃)₂.

What is the concentration of NaOH if 40.0 mL neutralizes 0.002 mol of H₂SO₄?

H₂SO₄ + 2NaOH: moles NaOH = 2 × 0.002 = 0.004 mol; [NaOH] = 0.004/0.040 = 0.1 M.
